content: HIT calculation refinements account for: pre-existing cross-immunity, age-structured contact patterns, spatial heterogeneity, and waning immunity. Basic formula (1 - 1/R0) assumes perfect, lifelong immunity and random mixing—rarely true. Age-stratified models weight immunity by contact rates (children have higher contacts for many diseases). Seroprevalence surveys measure actual population immunity. For SARS-CoV-2, evolving variants and waning immunity made stable herd immunity unlikely. Herd immunity should be viewed as a dynamic equilibrium requiring ongoing vaccination, not a one-time achievement.
